text: The patient had two colonic lesions, one of the cecum and the other in the distal rectum, up to one centimeter from the anal verge, and a single metastasis in the eighth segment. We planned a surgical resection of all lesions in a single stage using the da Vinci robot, as shown in the picture. First, resection of the liver metastasis, the robot coming from the head of the patient. second right hemicolectomy, robot coming from the upper right quadrant, and third an abdominal perineal resection with extraction of old surgical specimen from the perineal incision.

The patient is positioned in the left lateral decubitus with a high degree of hyperkyphosis. Due to limited space between the costal margin and the anterior superior iliac spine, the robotic arm cannot be docked. The surgical team switches to laparoscopy, performing an intraoperative ultrasound to guide the oncological resection of the liver metastasis, ensuring a margin of at least one centimeter. The resected specimen is placed inside a retrieval bag for delayed extraction.

The patient is repositioned for the second robotic docking, with the robot approaching from the right upper quadrant. Initially, the gastrocolic ligament is incised laparoscopically up to the right colon angle, marking the transverse colon resection point. The robot is docked, and dissection proceeds from middle to lateral, identifying the vascular plane between Gerota's fascia and Toldt's fascia. The second and third portions of the duodenum and the pancreatic fascia are recognized and sectioned. The ileocecal colonic vein and artery are isolated and sectioned between Hem-o-lok clips at their origin. The right mesocolon is transected in a corticranial direction along the lateral margin of the superior mesenteric axis. The right colic vessels and the right branch of the middle colic pedicles are sectioned at their origin. The transverse colon and omental loops are transected using a mechanical stapler. An intracorporeal lateral-lateral isoperistaltic anastomosis is performed with a mechanical stapler, and the enterotomies are closed with a double-layer suture: first with a running suture using PDS-III-O, followed by interrupted sutures using II-O Vicryl. The mesenteric defect is left open.

The surgeon begins the third phase of the procedure by incising the parietal peritoneum at the base of the sigmoid mesentery laparoscopically, proceeding medially to laterally between the Toldt and Gerota fascia until reaching the rectum. The robotic cart is docked from the left leg, and posterior dissection is performed between the fascia recti and Waldeyer's fascia down to the pelvic floor for total mesorectal excision. After visualizing the lateral ligaments bilaterally, anterior dissection continues along the rectovaginal septum. The sigmoid colon is sectioned with a mechanical stapler, and perineal surgery is performed to remove the surgical specimen, bringing out the right colon and liver metastasis through the perineal incision. The final view shows the patient's abdomen post-procedure.
